title: hieracium divisum subsp. commixtum
intro: Hieracium divisum subsp. commixtum, also known as Hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family with yellow or orange flowers. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Hawkweed have been associated with various health benefits, including wound healing, reduction of inflammation and pain, and antimicrobial activity. Hawkweed can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
